segment-text: Santiago Mitre and Mariano Llinas

INSPIRED BY ACTUAL EVENTS. ON DECEMBER 10th, 1983, ARGENTINA RESTORES ITS DEMOCRACY AFTER SEVEN YEARS OF A BLOODY DICTATORSHIP. THE FRAGILE DEMOCRACY TRIES TO COUNTER THE PRESSURE OF THE ARMED FORCES, WHICH REMAIN ACTIVE AND POWERFUL. THE COMMANDERS, WHO FEEL LIKE WINNERS OF A WAR AGAINST SUBVERSION, REFUSE TO BE CONSIDERED CRIMINALS, AND ONLY ACCEPT TO BE TRIED BY A MILITARY COURT.
2 INT - STRASSERA'S CAR - NIGHT 2

It's raining in Buenos Aires. Through the windshield, we see a downtown avenue. The only part of the driver we are able to see is his hand on the steering wheel, holding a cigarette. SUPERIMPOSE: "June 24, 1984" From the viewpoint of the car, we see a young couple rushing through the crowd. We don't know this yet, but the girl is VERONICA (17) and the man is her BOYFRIEND (29). They lean against a wall and kiss, then again on different walls, and finally say goodbye.
3 EXT - STREET / STRASSERA'S BUILDING - NIGHT 3
We follow the car driver from behind as he walks towards a building, the same one we saw the young girl walk into.
4 INT - ELEVATOR / STRASSERA'S BUILDING - NIGHT 4
For the first time we see the face of JULIO STRASSERA, 55, thick-framed glasses, bulky mustache, and bags under his eyes. He combs his wet hair.
5 INT - KITCHEN / STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 5
JULIO walks in very carefully. He dries his wet clothes with a towel. JULIAN (13), his son, pops up in his soccer shorts.
She came in and locked herself in her room.
Locked the door?
Yes. Just now. She just got here.
Their exaggerated seriousness turns the situation amusing.
He picked her up at Bettina's. They went to a bar. They spent quite a while there.
Which bar?
Plus Ultra. On Libertad and Arenales.
JULIO shakes his head. He doesn't like the bar thing.
And then?
I don't know... I took off...
What do you mean you took off?
I was out there for an hour... I left. (...) I don't like that guy.
Me neither.
SILVIA (50) shows up in her robe, ready for bed.
What are you two up to? What are you whispering about?
JULIAN answers normally, while JULIO stares at him, opening his eyes wide, trying to indicate that he should shut up.
We are worried about Veronica...
Why?
Her new boyfriend is a strange guy.
JULIAN notices his father is gesturing him to shut up, but it's already too late.
Strange? How do you know he's strange? How did you come to that conclusion?
SILVIA looks at JULIO, who intervenes.
Silvia, I have serious suspicions that this guy might be a service... They may want to use her eyes on me.
A service?
That's how you call a spy, Mom.
(Irritated) I know perfectly well what a service is. (She pauses, rubs her eyes) Julian, go to bed. Put your pajamas on. Don't sleep in your gym clothes.
JULIAN obeys and leaves. SILVIA looks at JULIO who behaves like a kid who was just caught being knotty.
I am waiting for your explanation.
Apparently they always meet at the Plus Ultra. Doesn't that ring any bells?
None.
State Intelligence, Silvia. The HQ is around the corner.
You're kidding, right? Are you following your daughter around?
No.
Julio. Have you had your daughter followed?
(pretending)
Julian ran into her.
Have you made your son follow your daughter?
(Hesitates) No, I'm telling you I haven't.
You're sick, Julio. We already checked, he is the cousin of that girl from school.
Yes... And she is the granddaughter of an aviator.
A civilian one, Julio. He's a commercial pilot.
At LADE. They're all kind of military.
(Shouting from his bedroom) They volunteered in the war! Father and grandfather!
Do you realize you're raising a lunatic?
6 STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - DAY 6

VERONICA, wearing her school uniform and a jean jacket, is putting on some lipstick in front of the mirror by the entrance door. JULIAN, wearing his blue blazer and gray trousers and carrying his backpack stops next to her, and looks at her.
Why are you wearing make-up for school?
Because I want to and because I can. We're in Democracy, right?
It's not allowed in my school.
How would you know? You never wore makeup in school...
In a quick movement, VERONICA tries to put lipstick on him. JULIAN dodges her swiftly; they struggle and laugh, until he walks out the door.
Stupid...
JULIAN leaves the door open. SILVIA and JULIO walk behind their daughter. SILVIA and JULIAN get in the elevator. JULIO and VERONICA take the stairs.
7 INT - STAIRWAY / STRASSERA'S BUILDING - DAY 7
JULIO and VERONICA are walking down the stairs. VERONICA lights a cigarette.
Shall we have lunch?
I can't.
I didn't say when...
Oh, I thought you meant today.
8 INT - ENTRANCE HALL / STRASSERA'S BUILDING - DAY 8
VERONICA says goodbye. JULIAN and JULIO look at each other.
9 INT - PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 9
JULIO walks into the Palace of Justice. A huge building, crowded with people rushing through. He walks up the stairs to the office of the Federal Prosecutor.
10 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 10
JULIO walks into his office. SUSANA (50), his secretary, is waiting for him by his desk.
Good morning. Bruzzo called.
SUSANA is strict, and has a piercing voice. JULIO takes off his coat and hangs it. He doesn't answer her.
Sir, Mr. Bruzzo called. He needs to talk to you urgently.
I heard you. And what happens if I don't want to talk to him?
Can you do that, sir?
Of course I can.
So, what shall I do if he calls again?
Hang up.
I can't do that, sir.
Not only you can, you have to. It's an order. You hang up Bruzzo. Do you understand? Because you work for me.
I'm a permanent staff member, sir. You can't fire me.
JULIO walks into his office, he stops before closing the door.
(Suddenly nice) Please, hang up on Bruzzo.
11 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 11

A cassette player is blaring the overture of Richard Wagner's "Tannhauser". JULIO smokes, his feet lie on his desk, which is covered with files. The door opens. It's SUSANA, who walks one step towards him but JULIO makes a soft gesture with his hand to stop her.
Don't ruin this moment. I beg you.
Bruzzo is coming over.
Tell me the truth... You hate me don't you?
No, if I hated you I wouldn't let you know he is, Sir.
JULIO gets up quickly.
You're right. (He checks his watch) Tell Bruzzo I had to go. I'm having lunch with my daughter.
12 INT - HALLWAYS / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 12

JULIO walks out. He sees BRUZZO approaching. He is tall, big, and walks around with confidence. JULIO walks in the opposite direction, around the main yard. He stops to spy on BRUZZO who walks into his office and, a few seconds later, comes out with SUSANA looking troubled. JULIO smiles and leaves.
14 ARCHIVE FOOTAGE 14

THE SCREEN IS COMPLETELY FILLED WITH THE BLACK AND WHITE IMAGES OF THE SPECIAL CONADEP TV BROADCAST. SUPERIMPOSE: July 17th, 1984. SUPERIMPOSE: CONADEP Special Program A JOURNALIST, EDUARDO VILLARROEL, INTRODUCES WHAT WE ARE ABOUT TO SEE:
(Archive) Right now, on channel 13, we are meeting the Minister of the Interior, Mr. Antonio Troccoli... and the Head of the Commission on the Disappeared,
JULIO, in OFF, protests.
National Commission on the Disappearance of Persons. Is it so hard to say it right?
SILVIA, off screen, hushes him. We see TROCCOLI speaking in a low definition, black and white image on an old TV set.
(On TV) In a few moments, you will see a report written by the National Commission on the Disappearance of Persons ...
There you go...
Whose members have been undertaking their task with integrity and a commendable patriotic element...
WE HEAR THE SOUND OF AN OLD BUTTON. THE OLD TV SET TURNS OFF. THE IMAGE FADES INTO A DOT OF LIGHT. IN OFF, WE HEAR JULIO AND SILVIA DISCUSSING.
What are you doing?
I can't stand him. That's not what he thinks.
Julio, can you turn the TV on? I want to hear what he says.
not
watching this phony.
Well, I am.
Me too, Dad.
The TV is back on. TROCCOLI is still speaking. THE TV IMAGE IS FULL SCREEN AGAIN.
(On TV) ...performing a task imbued with the lacerating drama, with wounds that will obviously be reflected in the testimonies you will hear...
You'll see... I give him less than five seconds.
(On TV) ...but what you're going to watch is only one side of the tragedy of violence in Argentina.
There he goes...
(On TV) The other face, the other side of it, started when subversion and terrorism washed up on the shores of Argentina, nurtured from distant borders...
There you go... This guy doesn't want the trial to happen.
...from remote geographies, with a handful of men who, leading a political project supported by terror...
Why don't they just put Admiral Massera on TV instead?
ONCE AGAIN, WE HEAR THE SOUND OF AN OLD BUTTON. THE IMAGE FADES TO A DOT OF LIGHT.
Turn it on, Julian.
15 INT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 15
JULIAN turns the TV on and sits next to SILVIA to continue watching. SILVIA doesn't like what she's hearing.
But, what is he saying?
I don't know what you expected...
The bell rings. VERONICA walks behind the couch.
It's Bettina, she needs some notes from class. I'll be right back.
JULIO stands up to reply, but she has already closed the door. JULIO tries to draw JULIAN's attention, but he's watching the TV attentively.
(On TV) Many generations were attracted to these Messianic projects and ended up pushed to the evil machinery of death and terror.
What?
(On TV) We must then realize that the Argentine society was overwhelmed and shocked by the subversive irruption...
(Shakes her head) Subversive iruption? This is like an announcement from dictatorship.
(On TV) It marginalized it, and demanded the eradication of violence. It gave unequivocal signs saying that this subversive outbreak should be ended...
(gets angry) !You were supposed to talk about the disappeared, Mr. Minister!
(to Silvia) See what I mean?
Hold on Dad, I'm watching this!
(On TV) They demanded the State to exercise its authority in order to put an end to these calamities that were unprecedented in the history of our country... But what they couldn't have foreseen...
(shouting at the TV) What is he saying?! You are blaming the victims,
JULIO has had enough, he stands up.
16 EXT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT / BALCONY - NIGHT 16

JULIO smokes, leaning against the balcony railing as he tries to peek down to the sidewalk. He sees his daughter crossing the street. She meets her BOYFRIEND, and they walk together. He holds her by the arm.
(To himself) Hmmm...

The couple wanders off. THE CAMERA LEAVES THEM AND PANS ON THE NEIGHBORING BUILDINGS. Julio looks at some apartment windows. Families, couples, people alone, almost everyone is watching the special CONADEP broadcast. We see a man crying. JULIO goes inside.
INT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT

JULIO walks back into the living room where SILVIA and JULIAN are, they are also moved in front of the TV. We see and hear the show, where the mother of a disappeared person (Estela de Carloto) is talking to the camera.
17 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 17
JULIO walks through the Palace of Justice, in a hurry. When he enters the office, SUSANA makes a gesture like saying "he is inside". BRUZZO emerges from Julio's office.
Can you do me a favor and get in here, Crazy?
18 INT - STRASSERA'S OFFICE / PROSECUTION - DAY 18
JULIO puts out a cigarette and lights another one almost in the same single move. BRUZZO waits calmly.
Crazy, can you sit down?
Why do you want me to sit?
We have to talk. There are things you need to know.
Look, Bruzzo. I don't want to argue with you. I respect you, I am grateful for my
fiocchi. But there is one thing I am not willing to be. You know what? You know what I'm not willing to be,
I can imagine, Julio.
(He builds up) A moron. Is that what you were thinking, Bruzzo? I'm not willing to be the moron who is called to be the face
nor anyone in the government believes in...
Julio...
BRUZZO doesn't say a word, he knows about his volcanic temper.

What happened? Are we suddenly living in Wonderland? All of the sudden dictators go to jail? Who is gonna put them in jail? Spiderman? Do you believe the Military Justice will allow Videla be sent to prison? They were part of the dictatorship, Bruzzo!
And what if the Civil Justice were to take over the trial?
JULIO stares at him, as if Bruzzo had said something really stupid.
Civil Justice? They call me Crazy but I am not stupid. That won't happen. Troccoli will let that happen?
I'm just telling you what is going to happen.

Oh, please, come on! Why don't you all speak clearly and tell me: "Julio, we want you to be the greatest moron in the Nation. To be that moron that says he's going to throw the dictatorship in the joint and then...Well... We couldn't make it... Maybe next time..." Well, you know what? Don't count me in.
JULIO takes a deep breath. BRUZZO remains calm.
I'll say it again: is it clear to you that if the judges take the trial, you won't be able to refuse, right?
Bruzzo, don't jerk around. You know as well as I do that it's not going to happen...
(With sudden authority) If the judges want it, it will happen. Is it clear or not?
Stop screwing around, Bruzzo.
(Raises his voice as he stands up) Is it clear or not?!
Brief pause. JULIO looks at him.
Yes, Bruzzo. I know perfectly well what my job is. I'm a prosecutor.
That's what I wanted to know. We will wait for news.
BRUZZO, stern, leaves. JULIO is left alone, somewhat worried.
19 INT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT / ELEVATOR - NIGHT 19
JULIO takes the elevator. He smokes. He seems absent-minded, and tired.
20 INT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT / STUDIO - NIGHT 20

JULIO goes to his studio. He puts a cassette tape in a rustic player. We hear a whirring version of Tannhauser. SILVIA walks in. She turns the volume down to the minimum. She looks at him for a few seconds.
Tell me, what happened?
Nothing happened.
(she knows) What did Bruzzo say?
Hmm. Don't want to talk about it.
They're doing it, right?
Don't want to talk about it, Silvia.
So they're doing it.
They CAN-NOT do it.
Yes they can. You know perfectly well that if the judges request the trial...
But they won't let them.
You don't know that.
Can we not talk about this,
And what subject do you want to talk about, then?
Nothing.
There's only one thing that isn't clear to me...
Silvia, please don't make me upset.
I don't understand if you get like this...
Silvia...
...because you're afraid...
...you are making me...
... they won't do it...
...very...
...or afraid...they WILL actually do it.
JULIO stops. He looks at her.
(Firm) Sometimes I think...that you're shitting your pants, that's what's going on. (...) What are you afraid of, exactly?
Everything, Silvia, everything. Of being used as a bargaining chip, of being blown away, of something happening to you... and... and...
There's a pause. SILVIA holds her gaze, understanding.
(Sentencing) ...and not being good enough for this trial?
JULIO doesn't answer.
(Certain) You'll be able, Julio. You can do this

JULIO turns up the volume of Tannhauser all the way up. We see a MILITARY JUDGE who finishes reading the sentence and signs it. He closes a light blue folder. A MONTAGE DESCRIBES THE PATH OF THE FILE WITH THE RULING OF THE MILITARY COURT.
22 INT - HALLWAYS / SUPREME COUNCIL OF THE ARMED FORCES - DAY 22
A YOUNG MILITARY OFFICER walks on a corridor carrying the light blue folder in his hands. He crosses another one. Have they decided?
23 INT - VAN / STREETS - DAY 23
The light blue folder with the ruling of the MILITARY COURT is on the back seat of a car that is on its way to the Palace of Justice.
24 EXT - FRONT DESK / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 24

The YOUNG MILITARY OFFICER walks with the sentence and approaches the Front Desk at the Palace of Justice. He waits with an arrogant attitude among several civilian court clerks. He gets to the desk. YOUNG MILITARY OFFICER
Good morning. I bring a ruling from the Supreme Council of the Armed Forces.
A FRONT DESK RECEPTIONIST looks at it and stamps it.
26 INT - OFFICE / FEDERAL COURT - DAY 26

Superimposed: FEDERAL COURT. The light blue folder with the ruling of the Military Court is on the table. Judge SOLANET -40 years old, with a long, carefully groomed mustache- is reading it. The atmosphere is tense.

(Reading. Serious) This Council informs that according to its investigation, the decrees, directives, orders, and military procedures to combat the stateless subversion were, in both their content and form, unobjectionable.
ASAD -big man, dark hair, combed-back- interrupts. His voice carries authority and calmness. You can sense a kind of
Unobjectionable?
Just like that.
TROTTA, thin and elegant, rushes him.
Keep reading...
The excesses were committed by the subordinates...and the court should investigate the wrongdoings of the alleged victims in order to pass sentence. LOSADA and D'AMICO are listening.
The wrongdoings of the victims.? Remarkable...
Enough, right?
They all look at each other. They're afraid.
Looks like it...
Shall we vote? Do we need to vote?
Nobody answers. We can see they are tense. VERGARA, bald, with a hard face, doesn't seem convinced.
I wouldn't take it so lightly. What do you mean, "lightly"?
I mean we have to be cold, think about the risks, and whether we can guarantee that this trial will follow procedure.
Why wouldn't it follow
There's enormous pressure.
What do you suggest?
SOLANET takes the military sentence in his hands again.
I'm sorry, but, does anyone really think we can leave it like this? They are blaming the victims!
No, no one does.
So what are we waiting for, gentlemen?
We must act, but not blindly.
(Raises his tone again) If we don't act blindly, and that means now, this trial will not take place. (Pause) We will give the military what they didn't give to their victims: a fair trial. OK?
They look at each other. They look happy despite the tension.
And the evidence?
The evidence are the prosecutor's job
And if they can't find any?
If they can't find any, we will have to absolve.
Shall we notify the prosecutor?
ASAD gets up and walks towards a typing machine.
Notify him.
27 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 27

SUSANA walks into the office, looking tense. She hands a memo to JULIO and leaves. JULIO reads the memo briefly. He puts the piece of paper down on the table. He lights a cigarette. He turns the music off. JULIAN looks at him.
Did something happen?
Yes.
What?
The trial. It's going to happen.
(thinks)
Are they going to put Videla in
I don't know. We have to try.
And Massera?
Yes, him too.
And all the others.
Yes. As many as possible.
(Pauses, thoughtful) And is that dangerous?
I guess it is a little bit dangerous.
They can kill you.
I guess they can kill me, yes.
(Meditates) And you can't say no?
No.
Julio stops looking at him and starts typing.
28 INT - STAIRWAY / RUSO'S APARTMENT - DAY

JULIO stops in front of a wooden door. It has a bronze plate that reads: Alberto Muchnik, Attorney. From the inside, we can hear someone clumsily playing a Schubert concert on a piano. He knocks on the door. The piano stops. We hear some steps. The door opens. On the other side, wearing a poncho -quite typical but also very elegant- is RUSO.
Mister Prosecutor.
Poor Schubert.
29 INT - RUSO'S APARTMENT - DAY

The apartment is warm, messy, somewhat bohemian, full of papers everywhere. Among the papers, an old upright piano. RUSO walks around holding a glass of wine. There is a large oxygen tube, and a mask next to RUSO's chair. Clearly he is sick. He looks at JULIO and laughs out loud.
(Laughing) They fucked you up alright, Crazy!
You're a smart guy, tell me... What's all this? Something is off.
You thought they were appointing you to sweep the dance floor, but in the end it looks like you're going to have to dance.
Ruso, don't pull my leg. There's something strange here. I can't do this.
Well, you had to start eventually, right?
RUSO returns to the living room, with a new glass of wine. He leans on the couch, looking at JULIO.
What is it you're scared of? That people will talk shit about you? That they accuse you of something? Or that they'll blow you up in the air?
That all of this turns out to be a trap.
He sits next to JULIO.

(Serious) Look ... I know what you mean. I've been around for fifty years watching how everything turns to shit. And, you know what? I've learnt only one thing: you are right. Everything is fucked up. Everybody is a pussy, a traitor, and an opportunist. I don't know who is worse. Every new government
says they are going to change things, and immediately gets crowded with the usual motherfuckers.
JULIO is about to light a cigarette but RUSO makes a sign for him to stop. JULIO stands up.

But, you know what? Sometimes something goes wrong, and there's a gap... a minimum one. Something that nobody sees. A crack. (He separates his fingers slightly) It doesn't last for long, it shuts right away. But, as long as the crack is open, you can do something. One must hurry, be alert. But, if you manage to do it before these motherfuckers notice it, you can change things a bit. You can do something good. Very good. Good things were done like that, all of the sudden. And they were done by the few people who realized this. The brave ones. That's how they made...
Silence.
Go on. They made what? Nothing... nevermind.
History. Was that what you were going to say? (RUSO doesn't respond) History was not made by guys like me.
(Smiles again) You don't say... And nonetheless, you are going to be the acting DA in the most important trial in Argentine history, you son of a bitch!
JULIO looks puzzled.
30 INT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT

The phone rings insistently. JULIAN, indifferent, is doing his homework on the kitchen table. VERONICA walks by, smoking, with her headphones on. We hear SILVIA's voice from the bathroom. SILVIA (OFF)
JULIAN, impassive, minds his own business. The phone rings on and on. JULIO walks in the apartment just when SILVIA is yelling again.
My God! Someone get that, it's
driving me crazy! JULIAN, can you get
JULIO answers the phone.
Hello.
On the other side of the line, a sinister voice talks in a menacing tone.
Who is this? VOICE (OFF) Is it you? Are you the father of Julian and Veronica?
Who is this? VOICE (OFF) The one who's gonna off you.
Uh-huh... VOICE (OFF) But, you know what?
What? VOICE (OFF) We're going to take care of your son first.
JULIO looks at his son, who is still just minding his own business.
Huh... VOICE (OFF) Your daughter looks quite nice, though, so we're gonna keep her.
(In a very low voice) You son of a nasty bitch, motherfucker... VOICE (OFF) Yes. So we can entertain ourselves.
VERONICA is reading comfortably on the sofa, listening to her Walkman.
Why don't you say that to my face? VOICE (OFF) Be careful, crazy asshole. So that you don't dig where it's in your best interest not to. So you don't have to regret it later.
Thank you very much. I will keep that in mind.
And he hangs up. He's a bit disturbed. He looks at JULIAN.
Who was that?
A friend.
JULIAN doesn't seem to believe him. JULIO hangs his coat.
Silvia! From now on, you and I are the only ones answering the phone here!
SILVIA comes over to him. She talks as if she is trying to hide something.
Who was that? The gentleman with the threats again? He's been calling all day. Nice voice.
32 INT - CAR / GARAGE - DAY 32

JULIO gets in his car. He is holding the car keys. He hesitates for a few seconds. He puts the keys in the ignition slowly. JULIAN looks at him from the distance. JULIO breathes, looks at the keys again. He is not sure about starting the car. Finally, he opens the door.
Let's take the subway.
EXT - JULIAN'S SCHOOL / PALACE OF JUSTICE SQUARE - DAY

JULIAN and JULIO walk out of the subway station. JULIO watches JULIAN as he goes into the school. Then he looks at the Palace of Justice, right across from the School. There is a small PARANOID SEQUENCE in which JULIO walks across the park. He crosses paths with several people; each one may be an assassin or a "service".
INT - HALLWAYS / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY
JULIO walks inside the Palace of Justice. Two suspicious men in civilian clothes intercept him. Julio gets frightened.
Mr. Strassera. Apologies for the interruption. I've been ordered by Mr. Bruzzo to report to you as Head of your Security Detail.
I didn't ask for any security detail.
I understand you and your family received death threats.
Young man, I appreciate it but I won't accept any security.
I understand, the threats are not important. If someone is going to hurt you, they won't tell you beforehand...and for that reason...
JULIO stands firm.
I don't want people from the intelligence services or the police near me and certainly not near my family.
With all due respect, I have just been at
your home, I introduced myself to Mrs.
because I am in charge of your security...
JULIO stops and looks at him.
What's your name?
Hormiga (Ant), Sir.
Your name, officer.
Hormiga, Sir.
Hormiga? Your last name is Hormiga?
Yes, with an O and two Gs. Ormigga. And this is Romero.
Well, forgive me, Hormiga. Have you been with my daughter? Let me say this upfront, she's a very independent girl, very zealous of her privacy.
I am aware, Sir.
(Interested) What are you aware of?
The safety of your family is... How to explain?... relevant to us. We had to conduct some inquiries.
Uh-uh... So?
Nothing to worry, Sir
(Thinks) Nothing? Nothing on my daughter's boyfriend, either?
Well, it's not our intention to go middle in the young lady's personal life.
Please, tell me what you know.
Nothing that can be a threat to your safety.
I see. Speak clearly. Let it go...
Engineer Padilla is a married man.
(Shocked) Married? Does my daughter know?
I don't believe so, Sir.
I'm sure she doesn't. Come with me.
JULIO gives HORMIGA a gentle tap on the shoulder.
34 INT - PROSECUTION'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 26
JULIO walks into his office and sees BRUZZO who is waiting for him next to SUSANA's desk.
What's up, Bruzzo? Thanks for the visit, but I have to push the most important trial since Nuremberg. So, please, let's not waste our time...
I came to offer you help.
I already got it. Atom Ant. Very nice.
I don't mean the security detail.
Thanks a lot. If I need any other kind of help I'll let you know.
Crazy, don't be stubborn. You can't
do this alone.
They walk into the office.
I won't be alone. I'll put a team together. I have the powers to put my team together, right?
It won't be so easy. There aren't many people in the Federal Justice who agree with what we're about to...
First: there is no 'We'. I am about to. Second: I already have people working for me, so...
I am just saying that there is little time and you won't be able to handle this on your own. And "on your own" includes your friend, the "theater guy".
"The theater guy", whom you are mocking, has more trial experience than you and me combined, Bruzzo. He's been working in the judicial system for 30 years. I will do this but I'll do it my way.
BRUZZO looks at him with an expression like saying "I know
If you do it your way, you'll never make it in time. I know what I'm saying. I have someone in mind.
Bruzzo stands up.
Don't send anyone. I will call my people: I'll call Telechea, I'll call Fat Salvador, I'll call Sturla...
35 INT - THEATRE - NIGHT 35
A couple of technicians are on the stage organizing the set while an ACTOR is practicing his lines (of the play "First Officer"). SOMI, his hair and beard slightly long, is sitting next to JULIO.
Sturla said no? His exact words were: "I won't get into this; I'm too old for this kind of thing".
Fat wimp. How old is he?
That old? Yes. He's old.
Hmm... Telechea? He said no.
Did he say why? He laughed. He said: "Crazy
Strassera wants to put the
face Hands of Stone Duran, on the
Very funny. Telechea never liked trouble. And Salvador?

I was lying down in the car, blindfolded and with my hands tied behind my back. They insulted me, I told them my child was coming, that I couldn't hold it any longer. I told them to stop, that it wasn't my firstborn and I knew it was time. They didn't do anything, the driver and the one next to him kept laughing. They told me it made no difference, they were going to kill me anyways, and my child too, what did I care? Finally, I don't even know how, I managed to take off my underwear, I really don't remember how. I screamed: 'It's coming, I can't wait anymore'. And, indeed, my baby girl was born, she was born. I was shouting them to stop the car. They finally stop on the verge of the road. My baby was healthy, she was very small. She was hanging from the umbilical chord. She th

fell off the seat; she ended up on the car floor. I asked them to please give her to me, to let me hold her. They didn't. They tied the umbilical chord and we kept going. Three minutes had gone by and my baby was crying. My hands were still tied behind my back, and I was blindfolded. They didn't want to give her to me. (...) We get to a different place, and they take me out of the
car. I walk into a large shop with marble countertops. They had placed my baby on one of the countertops. She
92 EXT - CASA ROSADA (GOVERNMENT HOUSE) - NIGHT 92

On one of the sidewalks of Plaza the Mayo, there is a parked Ford Falcon. We can see the Casa Rosada in the back. Suddenly, the car explodes. It starts catching fire while a thick black smoke comes out of the engine. Among the debris, there are photos of JULIO, newspaper articles about the trial and images of the president.
EXT - PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY
Several armed trucks and police men are guarding the front of the Palace of Justice.
Security at the trial has been doubled after the bomb that went off in Plaza de Mayo...
93 EXT - PAYPHONE / LAVALLE SQUARE - NIGHT 93
SEQUENCE OF CLOSEUPS OF TOKENS AND PAYPHONES. DIFFERENT VOICES SPREAD THE NEWS ON THE TRIAL.
The aggressive strategy of the
INT - RADIO STATION - NIGHT
The defense counsel basically blamed the witness for their own abduction, proving that the defense...
INT - TV STUDIO - NIGHT
The defense seemed to suggest that the former detainee was responsible for his detention, and even for the torture inflicted on him.
94 INT - COURTROOM / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY 94
ADRIANA is finishing her deposition.

(Continues) A month later I was released...we lost our jobs, we lost our house, we lost our friends, we lost everything, we had to leave the country. They managed to terrorize me, Your Honor. Fortunately, they did not manage to terrorize the people. There were relatives, there were mothers, there were grandmothers, who faced them, Your Honor. And today, thanks to them, I'm here demanding justice. I think I don't have anything else to add...
Prosecutor. Any questions?
JULIO looks serious but he is moved. LUIS holds his head between his hands.
No questions, Your Honor.
Does the defense want to make any questions? Nobody answers.
Your deposition is over. Thank you very much.
ADRIANA stands up, still shaking. The courtroom is silent, moved.
95 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE - NIGHT 95

FEDERICO, MACO, ISABEL, are working, sorting out the material for the next hearing. A phone rings. SUSANA, already wearing her coat and bag, makes a gesture like saying, "I'm off" and doesn't answer the phone. JUDITH picks up the phone.
It's for Luis. Your mom.
LUIS looks at JULIO in shock.
Let's see...
LUIS goes to the other room, sits on the chair and answers the phone.
Hello Mom. Fine, I'm very well. We had a good hearing today. Oh, you heard it. On the radio? Of course I knew, I didn't know you were listening to it every day. Really?...And what do you think?
The first one to lean through the door is JUDITH, paying attention to the phone call.
JULIO picks up another phone and starts listening too.
Is it true, everything she told?
Yes, it's true.

How can someone be so cruel? Showing no mercy to a pregnant woman... making her suffer so much... Not caring even about a baby. Dreadful, honestly. Dreadful. I never thought I would hear something so...dreadful.
Me neither, Mom.
LUIS can't help himself; he has tears in his eyes.
Oh, Luis. I know I told you ugly things. It's not easy for someone who has her own ideas to see her son doing things she strongly disagrees about.
Well... MOTHER (OFF) It's the way I've always thought. Because of my Education, my religion, the people I know...because I've always respected the Army... But now... I think you're right...
I'm right? MOTHER (OFF) Yes... Videla should go to prison.
LUIS can't hold it anymore and starts crying.
Yes, mom... He should go to prison. MOTHER (OFF) Are you crying?
No.
LUIS takes the phone to his ear again. JULIAN walks in and observes the situation.
(in a low voice, to Julio) Why is this one crying?
JULIO makes a gesture telling him it's not important, and softly pushes him towards a different area of the office.
INT - RUSO'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
A vinyl record is playing an opera. JULIO returns from the kitchen with a plate of fruit. There are leftovers on the table. RUSO looks deteriorated yet excited with his friend's visit.
That's why the case of Hilda Cardozo is crucial: she was detained in Rosario, went through ESMA, and disappeared in Cordoba. Interconnection between the different branches.
JULIO sits down and starts peeling off a tangerine as he listens to him.
I got it, Ruso.

And the Bettinis. You have all the crimes in one singe family. The entire state terrorism apparatus, condensed. First, the son is murdered, then the father is kidnapped, and then the grandmother, now for financial reasons.
Exactly.
The kidnapping is an excess by itself the fact that it was an old lady only adds to the horror. And on top of that, they did it to steal their assets.
JULIO hands him the tangerine, tries to tone down the conversation.
Relax, Ruso. We are doing good.
Hmmm.
Seriously, we're doing good.
I don't like to hear you say that.
That we're doing good?
Not because you aren't. I just don't like to hear you say 'we're doing good'. You don't have to think you are doing good...because right when you believe you're doing good...
Don't be an asshole. You know I don't like to say that: I'm even more superstitious than you, but...
You have to be sharper than ever now. (Takes a deep breath) Up until now: you got the attention and you got the
emotion. Perfect. But now...now comes the ESMA, now comes Massera, Julio. And that one is crazy, perverse, dangerous.
A cough interrupts him. He choaks for a moment.
Are you OK?
RUSO is short of breath, he suffocates.
No. (Joking) But your not OK either. Understand?
JULIO comforts him.
OK.
Julio, listen to me. The ESMA is hard. I know the turf. ESMA is tough.
I know, I know.
(Softer, with a weak voice) No. You don't know. Listen to me...
RUSO keeps talking, JULIO listens.

They had Air Force and Prefecture operatives there. Five thousand people disappeared there...Five thousand there alone. Massera himself led the operations...to show them how it was done. (Thinks) Wait for me here. (Stands up) I'll show you...
JULIO is left alone, pensive.
INT - CAR - NIGHT
JULIO puts the key in the ignition and hesitates before starting the car. He remains thoughtful. Afraid. Finally, he turns the key. The car starts.
100 INT -STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT 100

An envelope with Navy letterhead is lying on the dining room table. In the background we hear a door opening and SILVIA, JULIAN and VERONICA walk in. They leave their stuff at the entrance. VERONICA walks by but stops when she sees the letter. She grabs it.
Mom. There's a letter here: From the Argentine Navy, Mom
What's it doing there?
I don't know, someone left it here.
(Hiding her fear) Call Hormiga.
VERONICA does that while SILVIA reads the letter addressed to JULIO.
Are they going to kill us?
No, honey.
SILVIA doesn't answer, she is worried. The typed letter announces a new death threat to Julio.
101 INT - CAR / BUS TERMINAL - DAY 101

The entire family is in the car. JULIO, SILVIA, JULIAN and VERONICA. JULIO stops the car at one of the bus terminal entrances. Behind them, in another car, we see HORMIGA and ROMERO, who hurry up to help them with their things.
It's only two weeks.
You said one...
Doesn't matter what he said. Let's do this quick.
I'm not scared.
If someone is going to do something to you, they don't threaten you, Dad. Everyone knows that.
They broke into our home, and I have a hearing in an hour. Please, I beg you.
HORMIGA y ROMERO observe the family discussion a bit surprised.
It's the most difficult day of the trial. The last thing I want is for something to happen to you. Two weeks. Until we are done with ESMA...That's all I'm asking. Until we're done with ESMA!
Everyone remains silent.
Let's go, kids...Let's go.
102 INT - CAR / BUS TERMINAL - DAY
JULIO speeds up and looks at this family with their bags through the rear view mirror.
103 EXT - LAVALLE ENTRANCE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY
JULIO faces a female TV reporter who intercepted him on his way to the Court.
Sir, today all the media is reporting a new threat you have received. This time it was a letter and in your home.

It's a meaningless event; I believe it was mostly meant to intimidate the witnesses and not so much myself. And it's a rather clumsy action, because if they did anything to me, there's always going to be someone who will replace me...
So you believe the threats. Have you reported them? Is there someone investigating them?
The corresponding court will manage the investigation.
According to the information the threat was a letter with the Argentine Navy's letterhead. Can you confirm that?
I have to get ready for the hearing, Miss. Thank you for your concern.
JULIO leaves. The TV REPORTER goes on.

That was Prosecutor Strassera, who today faces what is likely the hardest moments in the trial of the former commanders: the testimonies of people detained at the Navy School of Mechanics, probably the largest clandestine detention center...
104 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY
On the table, a photo of the ESMA facade, maps of the compound, official Navy documents, photos of repressors.
Look, I don't want to lie to you. Everything you brought in is very good, but your case is...how can I say this...special.
BARRASA, a robust, balding man with a mustache is sitting across the table, looking at LUIS and smoking.
Let's see... don't take this the wrong way. Your situation is different from the other detainees. And the defense...
(Gently, but sounding nervous) I don't follow you, Sir. What do you mean by "different"?
Let me be clear. We are trying to look out for you... I'm trying to prevent...
Tell me, why is it different? Let's see.
Leaning against the wall, JULIO just observes the situation.
The defense will try to prove that you weren't a victim, but an employee of the ESMA.
But it's the truth... I worked for them... They forced me to work for them...
Precisely. And we understand the context, the torture, the threats... Our intention in this meeting is to warn you...
LUIS is looking at JULIO trying to get him involved, but he keeps aside.
What I mean is... There are other ways, we could submit your deposition in writing so we don't risk difficult questions...
(firmly) I can answer any question.
Yes, the idea is not to put you at risk.

But I have already risked myself, a lot... taking this evidence out... precisely for this, for this trial... (He looks for the words) They kidnapped me, they tortured me, they forced me to rat out comrades, some of them disappeared because of me... then they forced me to work against my will, forging documents and IDs. (...) I spent many years lying to the military and lying to myself. I am not going to lie now. I'm a victim and I must be heard.
JULIO jumps in, finally.
It is crystal clear.
105 INT - COURTROOM - DAY

JULIO and LUIS are on the desk assigned to the prosecution. LUIS is shaking his head, saying no. Something is not going right. BARRASA is on the witness stand. GONZAGA (Massera's defender) is cross-examining the witness.
Can you describe what that compensation was?
Money to buy a few items and visits to my family...
What was the reason, in your case, for granting you to visit your family?
I believe it was part of a compensation.
Let the witness answer if that compensation he just described could be considered as a retribution for his work at ESMA?
The courtroom is silent, the atmosphere is tense. BARRASA hesitates.
I was forced to use my knowledge for a task that I knew perfectly well to be illegal, the forgery of Ids. And I was forced to do it.
According to the witness' statements, every detainee at ESMA was forced to perform tasks, yet not all of them were allowed to leave for family visits, as the witness was.
BASTERRA looks in the direction of LUIS and JULIO, who are also tense.
As far as you knew, did your exits and entries had the same characteristics as those of the rest of the ESMA staff, such as NCOs, or troops?
Not at first. But later on, yes, since I had a badge... It was quite similar...
LUIS leans forward to JULIO.
They are trashing him.
JULIO asks to speak, somewhat hesitating.
Your Honor. The evidence provided by the witness is eloquent enough to make the court put a stop to this incredible harassment from the
defense...to prove if he was a slave or not...
(Stops him) Mr. Prosecutor. Are you suggesting that the defense should be denied their right to cross-examine?
No, but these questions are biased... and...
Mr. Prosecutor. The defense has the right to ask the questions they deem necessary...and is the court's right to decide whether they are appropriate or not.
Precisely...
BARRASA is alone on the stand, looking at both sides.
106 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - NIGHT

MACO, concerned, walks into the office. LUCAS, ISABEL and JUDITH are sitting in their places, not talking. They are eavesdropping on a discussion between LUIS and JULIO in the other room. They all look at MACO, who makes a gesture asking what is going on. FEDERICO replies with another gesture indicating a fight.
It looked as if they were questioning a suspect. If you heard the trial today, it looked like the ESMA was a club where detainees were just strolling around...
ISABEL, shy, closes the door. A few seconds later, FEDERICO opens it again.
INT - JULIO'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - NIGHT
JULIO smokes, looking serious, indecipherable. For the first time, he seems calmer than LUIS.
...If I have to start crying for every witness that becomes complicated, for every question from the judges, or for every time the defendant's pressure, I'd have to retire... It's their job...
And ours is to win this trial! To win it at the indictment, at the sentence, and in the streets.
In the streets? Which streets? Talcahuano or Parana?
Don't patronize me.
You need to understand the limits of our job,
(Calming down) Witnesses are heroes, and we have to take care of them...
We are officials of the Judiciary. Officials! We don't get involved with witnesses, we don't get involved with judges, and we don't let anyone pressure us. Is that clear?
You know better than anyone, some officials wash their hands off- and played dumb during the entire dictatorship...

JULIO's face turns. He seems almost offended. Or about to have a violent outburst, but he refrains himself. He can see the prosecution kids in the next room, surprised, looking at them. JULIO slowly approaches the door and closes it.
For instance... who played dumb?
You know perfectly well what I'm talking about.
No, I don't. Explain.
I mean that being an official means nothing if you don't...
(Interrupting) No, don't beat around the bush. What are you talking about, Luis?
... About the fact that even if you are an official you have to take risks, and not be condescending.
So I don't take risks, and I am condescending?
I didn't say that. But it's not time to...
(interrupts him) Yes! You said I don't take any risks, that I am condescending, that I am useful to the reigning power, and that I've spent the dictatorship playing dumb!
I never said that, Julio. I'm talking about something different.
You're inches away from throwing the files of the habeas corpus I didn't investigate on my face,
I'm talking about something else!
No. !You're not talking about anything else! I understand perfectly what you're talking about. What the fuck do you think we were doing during the dictatorship? Partying in Punta
money? Being protected by the fact that we belonged to a noble family? With an uncle who's a Colonel? With a mother who went to mass with Videla?
108 INT - PROSECUTOR'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - NIGHT
The atmosphere is still very tense. Everyone is eavesdropping by the door. ISABEL and LUCAS look at the phone ringing. A silence. Also inside JULIO's office.
It's death threat hour. Who is going to get that?
109 INT - JULIO'S OFFICE / PALACE OF JUSTICE - NIGHT
The phone keeps ringing, insistently, as JULIO unloads.
I can't answer for my family, but I can answer for myself.
Fuck you, Moreno Ocampo!
Fuck you, Strassera!
I can answer for myself too! (Picks up the phone) Hello. Strassera. (...) OK... OK.... OK.... (...) OK... OK... OK...
LUIS stares at him, still furious.
OK...Tomorrow...OK.
JULIO hangs up the phone. He looks up. He and LUIS exchange looks for a few seconds.
Bruzzo.
What.
I don't know.
JULIO walks through the two offices. The kids say goodbye, quietly.
INT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT

The elevator stops. JULIO searches for his keys inside his pocket and opens the door. He turns on the light by the entrance, as he notices a sound that surprises him. It's actually a whisper, in which there's laughter, as if it were a gathering. His family hasn't left, they're all sitting around the table, having dinner. HORMIGA is with them.
Julio! I know, I know... We didn't go.
JULIO approaches. VERONICA hugs him and kisses him.
We couldn't bear it! We want to be with you, no matter what.
(To Hormiga)
We'll talk to you later.
JULIO sits at the table. SILVIA grabs his hand.
(In her ear) You're an asshole.
I love you too.
INT - MEETING ROOM - DAY
JULIO is alone sitting at a big meeting table. He's tense. BRUZZO suddenly walks in, he is restless. The scene is almost the opposite of the ones from the beginning.
The first thing I have to say is congratulations...
Thank you.
What you did brought hope to many in this context. Not just me, everybody is very proud of the work you're
Thank you.
The President himself told me what you are doing is an example. n Historical.
Tell the president I appreciate it.
JULIO looks sharply at him. There's a silence. BRUZZO suddenly stands up, energetic.
Coffee? A nice coffee...
BRUZZO stands up, goes up to a phone.
Can I have two coffees
Can you tell me what you want? Can you just give me the bad news you have to give me? What did Troccoli ask you for?
BRUZZO looks at him, somewhat confused. He slowly walks closer to JULIO.
Uhhh....Look...There's an issue that's been bugging me
and I need your help ...
My help?
Yes, it's a delicate matter...
BRUZZO closes the drapes on the window that leads to the hallway, he sits close to JULIO, who is listening very serious.
111 INT - RESTROOMS / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY
LUIS is nervous, talking to the closed door of a bathroom stall.
Can you tell me exactly what
Bruzzo said in the meeting?
I already told you three times, Luis. He asked me to...how should I put it...
"to back down"
Let's see...You're saying that, but I need to understand whether this is an assumption you made or an objective fact.
I wasn't born yesterday, Luis. If Bruzzo summons me to his office and says to me that...
Right... Says what?...Tell me what he said to you, word by word....
I'm in the toilet...
Maybe you are assuming he tried to said something, and it wasn't really like that...
Luis...
Can you please tell me what Bruzzo said!
He told me to be careful with our conviction requests. (repeats bitterly) "Careful"...
What does it mean, 'careful'?
For example, right now I'm carefully wiping my ass.
We hear a toilet flushing. JULIO opens the door.
And what did you tell him?
I told him that he would only learn about the conviction requests at the indictment hearing...and that the judicial is independent.
JULIO washes his hands, then combs his hair.
And what did he say?
He said that if the Judiciary doesn't act responsibly, it could be worse for democracy ... And that the Air Force must come out
(unbelieving) Did he say that?
More or less...
How so? Did he say "The Air Force..."?
(Cutting in) No. He didn't say that, but he meant it...
So the words "careful" and "Air Force" weren't in the same sentence.
JULIO steps out of the stall and LUIS stares at him.
Trust me: we are on our own.
LUIS doesn't quite follow. JULIO pats him in the back, as if consoling him.
We tried. You did a hell of a job.
INT - COURTROOM / PALACE OF JUSTICE - DAY
JULIO and LUIS sit behind their desk. They observe the DEFENSE ATTORNEYS, holding back their anger.
(to LUIS, in a low voice) Look at those sons of bitches, the Air Force's attorneys. They're mocking us.
JULIO, furious, makes an obscene gesture, consisting of introducing his index finger in a phallic way into a whole made by the index and thumb of his other hand.
Julio, stop...
Off screen, we hear the voice of the AIR FORCE ATTORNEY
Your Honor, could you request the Chief
himself from making insulting gestures to the Defense?
JULIO looks at him, biting on his anger.
115 INT/EXT - CAR / STREET - DUSK

LUIS looks worried. He's driving his old Dodge on the street. It's springtime: the Lapacho trees are blossoming. He stops at a traffic light. Two girls on a motorcycle stop next to him. They look at him, smiling. LUIS looks at them, not understanding.
It's you, right?
Me?
I mean, are you that prosecutor?
Yes, it's me... I'm the prosecutor.
Too bad you're a lefty.
The woman winks. The car drives away. LUIS freezes, he doesn't react. The cars behind him start honking. WE START HEARING "FUGA Y MISTERIO", BY ASTOR PIAZZOLLA. A voice says:
These companies, which care about the country, present Bernardo Neustadt and
"Tiempo Nuevo"
INT - TV STUDIO - DAY
BERNARDO NEUSTADT is interviewing JAIME GONZAGA, Massera's defense attorney. The image fills the screen.
What would you say today to someone who is watching the trial, and hears day-in, day-out, that Admiral Massera is a murderer?
I would tell them to think about how
years ago. To think about the guerrillas. To think about
Isabel. About the Argentina of the

ask that person: What would have happened if the Admiral had lost that war? If the government was in the hands of the montoneros? Where would president Alfonsin be? And the judges? They would be in jail, or executed.
INT - DINING ROOM / LUIS' MOTHER APARTMENT - NIGHT
LUIS speaks from the dining room, holding a glass of wine.
That's not right.
What?
What he is saying.
What do you want him to say? He is Massera's lawyer, he has to defend him.
LUIS comes closer.
They can't keep saying those things in that show.
(Casually) You guys go then...
What?
You guys should go...
No way. Julio hates him.
Julio is crazy.
I'll talk to him, we can't leave that spot to them.
LUIS sits on a sofa, his image reflects on the TV screen. We see LUIS reflection on the TV screen.
117 INT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
A phone rings in the kitchen. JULIO approaches and answers.
(Monotonous) Hello... no... no... no... I said no. What do you mean, why? Don't you think "just because" is enough? Ok. Neustadt was the
propaganda. That guy is just as guilty as the commanders. (...) Do whatever you want. Bye.
JULIAN and SILVIA arrive, she looks at him without understanding.
Moreno Ocampo. He wants to be on TV. The Neustadt show.
What did you say to him?
Nothing. He can do whatever he wants. He
is a big boy.
They're going to rip him a new one. That's what they do.
JULIAN spies the conversation from a side when the phone rings again. He rushes to pick it up.
Hello. Yes, that's correct. Yes, he is here. (He opens his eyes wide. It's important) I'll put you through.
JULIAN goes up to his parents.
Dad. Telephone.
No.
It's not Moreno Ocampo.
JULIAN looks at him, serious: he puts his hand together in the typical way Alfonsin does.
INT / EXT - CAR / STREETS - NIGHT
JULIO sits on the passenger seat, seemingly worried. HORMIGA starts driving in one direction.
You know where we're going?
Yes, sir.
Oh, you do? How do you know?
I was in the minister's security detail.
You don't say... Well, I'm not meeting him.
That's clear.
What's clear?
That you're not meeting the minister.
And who am I meeting, then?
I don't know.
You don't know?
It's not my place to know.
But you do...
I can't answer that.
You can at least tell me how you know.
Neither. It's delicate.
What's delicate, Hormiga?
The way I got my information.
You're worrying me, Hormiga. You tell me right now how do you know, or...
(Admitting) Julian told me. He wants a thorough report.
119 INT / EXT - CAR / ELEGANT BUILDING / PARKING - DAY

HORMIGA and JULIO arrive at the building on a downtown street. Standing by the door, a SECURITY GUARD in black suit and sunglasses awaits. As he sees them coming, he gestures to them to drive into the garage.
121 INT - ELEGANT APARTMENT - DAY
JULIO walks into a spacious apartment. A MINISTER welcomes them.
(Friendly) Mr. Prosecutor, apologies for the inconveniences of this meeting, but I'm sure you'll understand that we couldn't do
this in the Government House.
I understand perfectly, Minister.

The MINISTER leads JULIO to an office. He opens the door of a studio with a desk covered with books and invites JULIO to enter. BRUZZO is sitting on a chair, looking at him from a distance. BRUZZO gets up. He indicates where to sit. A hoarse voice speaks off frame:
Counsel Strassera, thank you very much for coming.
INT - CAR - NIGHT
JULIO looks out the car window, thoughtful.
INT - KITCHEN / STRASSERA'S APARTMENT - NIGHT
JULIO is stirring a cup of instant coffee while telling SILVIA about the meeting. SILVIA behaves as if she is trying to decipher a secret message.
It was brief. Fifteen minutes, or less.
And you told me Bruzzo was there.
But he didn't say anything.
And why was he there then?
I don't know. Maybe at Troccoli's request.
OK, and what did the president say?
He said 'I don't have any instructions for you'.
Hmmmm...
Indeed...
Isn't it strange, that the President of the Nation calls me to a meeting and then he has nothing to say? It's strange, isn't it? The President of the Nation summons you to a secret
meeting to tell you he has
Very strange.
So?
We talked about the trial.
But what exactly? Can you be more specific?
He talked about the testimonies. Some of them in particular. That he was moved. That he had met some of the witnesses. He did all the talking.
SILVIA maintains her questioning attitude.
And you didn't tell him anything?
I did... I asked what he expected from the trial.
(Excited) Good. Very good. What did he say?
That's when he said 'I don't have any instructions for you.
SILVIA makes a gesture of disappointment. She shakes her head. Julio shrugs.
That's it? He must have said something else.
In the end, when I was leaving, he said something...
(paying a lot of attention) There it is: What did he say?
He said: "I am looking forward to hear your indictment".
He said that?
Yes.
SILVIA thinks for a few seconds.
Hmmm.
Crystal clear.
What do you think?
Separation of powers.
It's perfect.
What do you think?
(she gets enthused) I think that you will have to write a nice indictment, a hell of an indictment. It needs to be striking. Because many things depend on that. EVERYTHING depends on that.
OK...I get it...please, don't make me even more
nervous. He steps out to the balcony, escaping.
124 EXT - STRASSERA'S APARTMENT / BALCONY - NIGHT
SILVIA follows JULIO. She approaches him from behind.
Julio, I'm going to tell you something I never told you before. And the fact that I never said it says a lot. And
I want to say it now, before anyone...
Come on, Silvia.
I'm proud of you!
You are not helping me with that.
Well, I don't care. This beat up country needs people like you.
